title: Harden state-root migration against ancestor path replacement
goal: Bind reviewed Bureau state-root migration and rollback effects to stable filesystem anchors so replacing or symlinking an ancestor directory between review, preflight and rename cannot redirect an otherwise valid operation.

acceptance.assertion: A deterministic synthetic test documents whether replacing, renaming or symlinking a source, destination or reference-root ancestor between review, preflight and effect can redirect the current path-based operation; no exploitability claim is made without a reproduction.
Plans and receipts bind the relevant root and parent directory identities, including device and inode where supported, and fail closed when any bound anchor changes before an effect.

Linux effects use validated directory descriptors and descriptor-relative no-follow operations such as openat2, renameat or renameat2 where needed, rather than re-resolving reviewed absolute paths at the effect boundary.
Rollback applies the same anchor, no-follow, all-entry preflight and compensation guarantees as forward migration.
Unsupported kernels or filesystems produce a typed fail-closed result or an explicitly weaker read-only diagnostic path; no silent fallback weakens mutation safety.

Tests cover ancestor replacement, symlink substitution, cross-device boundaries, concurrent topology drift and interrupted effects; a bounded proof uses only temporary synthetic roots and performs no active Bureau state migration.

metadata.source: deterministic synthetic reproduction, PR #542 and immutable Bureau runtime readback

observation.before_fix: On unchanged main, replacing the state-root ancestor after the last identity check caused Bureau to report success while moving a replacement object and leaving the reviewed object behind.
observation.after_fix: Plans and receipts schema 2 bind directory and top-level entry device/inode identities; apply and rollback use no-follow descriptor-relative operations and reject or compensate reproduced ancestor, symlink, topology and entry-replacement interference.
observation.safety_boundary: All adversarial effects used temporary synthetic roots. No active Bureau state migration, rollback, recovery or deletion was executed.

residual_tasks.OPERATOR-MACHINE-READABILITY-V1-T015: Crash and power-loss recovery journal between effects and final receipt.
